turns.content: Hey, I'm back in the game after five years and trying to fix our CI/CD pipeline that's failing because it can't reach external APIs. I thought using reqwest with a simple string config for the proxy settings would be enough, so I wrote this: let client = reqwest::ClientBuilder::new().proxy(reqwest::Proxy::all("http://my-proxy.local:3128")).build();

That looks like a good start, but you're making a common mistake when you just pass a string. reqwest doesn't automatically parse that URL into the right internal structure for your proxy logic. You need to use the specific Proxy enum variant and then attach it via the builder method on the Client type instead of passing raw text directly.
Oh, I see. So I should be constructing a new Proxy object first? Like using reqwest::Proxy::from_url("http://my-proxy.local:3128") and then calling .proxy() on the builder?

Exactly, you're getting closer. But there's a nuance here for your corporate setup since it might require SOCKS5 or have specific NO_PROXY environment variables active. The reqwest client builder accepts either a direct Proxy struct instance or an Option<Proxy> depending on whether you want the proxy to apply globally or selectively. Since you mentioned corporate proxies, are you also setting up any special handling for localhost requests that should bypass the proxy using env vars?
